text:
Rate Adjustment Formulas
Initial Rate (after warmup)
initial_rate = 2 x conn_rate_core multiplier x num_cores
Examplial ces 2x4.0% 8 = 64 connections/sec
Decrease Rate (stricter limiting)
new_rate = last_applied_rate × 0.95
Example: 64-60 → 57-54→ 51→...
Always decreases by 5% per adjustment.
Increase Rate (more permissive)
Scalar depends on how much CPU headroom exists:
CPU Idle %
Scalar
Rate Change
< 30%
× 1.05
+5%
30-40%
× 1.10
+10%
40-50%
× 1.15
+15%
50-70%
× 1.20
+20%
= 70%
× 1.30
+30%
More idle CPU - faster recovery.
Queue Depth
max_queue_depth = rate_per_sec x queue_size_secs
Example (rate = 64, queue_size_secs = 19)
ax_queue_depth = 64 × 19 = 1216 connectior
State Machine
Inactive
- Server doesn't support feature
_ initi) succeeds
WaitForwarmup - 60 seconds (default)
I warmup_period_secs elapsed
NeedsEnablement
I set initiat rate
EnabledAtDefaultRate
NeedsToDecrease
Ratelimit

RateLimit
EnabledAtCustom
Rate
o (continues adjusting)
Policy Tick Lifecycle
Every tick (default: 1000ms)
• WARMUP CHECK
- If elapsed time < warmup_period_secs (60s) → skip tuning
→ STABILIZATION CHECK
- If time since last tune < stabilization_interval_secs (5s) → skip tuning
• POLL PHASE
- Read CPU times from /proc/stat
Calculate idle% since last tick
- Execute serverstatus (max 500ms timeout)
- Calculate connection rate since last tick
- Classify CPU overload (None/Mild/Severe)
- Classify connection rate (None/Low/Medium/High)
Check if rate limiting occurred (rejected/queued > 0)
→ TUNE PHASE
- If NeedsEnablement:
- Set burst_capacity_secs
• Set max_queue_depth
- Enable rate limiter
→ EnabledAtDefaultRate
- If NeedsToDecreaseRateLimit:
- new rate = rate x 0.95
- Update rate limit
→ EnabledAtCustomRate
If NeedsToIncreaseRateLimit:
- scalar = 1.05 to 1.30 (based on idle%)
new_rate = rate x scalar
- Update rate limit
→ EnabledAtCustomRate
---
